name: refactor extract leaves unused imports in source file
acceptanceCriteria: After extracting a function, any imports in the source file that are no longer referenced by remaining code are removed
Extraction does not remove imports that are still used by other code in the source file
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file (`run.ts`) with a function (`resolvePlan`) that is the sole consumer of certain type imports (`BacklogItem`, `PlanPhase`)
2. Run `assist refactor extract src/commands/backlog/run.ts resolvePlan src/commands/backlog/resolvePlan.ts --apply`
3. Inspect the source file after extraction

**Expected:** Imports that are no longer used by any remaining code in the source file are removed during extraction.

**Actual:** The `import type { BacklogItem, PlanPhase } from "./types"` was left behind in `run.ts` despite `resolvePlan` being the only function that referenced those types. This caused a lint failure (`noUnusedImports`).

name: backlog add --json fails when echo passes JSON with \n literals
acceptanceCriteria: An agent can add a backlog item with multi-line description fields in a single attempt without hitting a JSON parse error
description:
**Repro:**
1. An agent generates JSON with `\n` escape sequences in string values
2. Pipes it to `assist backlog add --json` using `echo`
3. `JSON.parse` throws `SyntaxError: Bad control character in string literal`

**Expected:** The agent successfully adds a backlog item on the first attempt.

**Actual:** The command fails, requiring the agent to retry with a different shell approach (heredoc).

name: refactor extract adds unused barrel export to index.ts
acceptanceCriteria: refactor extract only adds a barrel export when the function was already exported via the barrel before extraction
Extracting a non-barrel-exported function does not modify index.ts
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file with a private helper function (e.g. `handleIncompletePhase` in `resolvePhaseResult.ts`) that is only used internally within the same directory
2. Run `assist refactor extract <source> <functionName> <destination> --apply`
3. Check the directory's `index.ts` barrel file

**Expected:** The extracted function should only be exported from the new file and imported where needed. No barrel export should be added unless the function was already publicly exported.

**Actual:** The extract command unconditionally adds a barrel export to `index.ts` (e.g. `export { handleIncompletePhase } from "./handleIncompletePhase"`), even when the function is only consumed internally. This causes knip to flag an unused export.

name: refactor extract copies import paths verbatim into destination
acceptanceCriteria: When extracting to a different directory, import paths in the destination file are rewritten relative to the destination
Build and type checks pass on the extracted file without manual fixup
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file `src/commands/registerBacklog.ts` that imports from `"./backlog"` (a barrel)
2. Run `assist refactor extract src/commands/registerBacklog.ts registerItemCommands src/commands/backlog/registerItemCommands.ts --apply`
3. The extracted file lands inside `backlog/` but still contains `from "./backlog"`

**Expected:** Import paths in the extracted file are rewritten relative to the destination directory (e.g. direct imports from `./add`, `./init`, `./list`).

**Actual:** Import paths are copied verbatim from the source file, producing an unresolvable `./backlog` import that fails build and type checks.

name: Concurrent assist next agents compete for the same in-progress item
acceptanceCriteria: An in-progress item actively being worked on by another agent is skipped
An in-progress item with no active agent is still resumable
Two concurrent assist next invocations work on different items
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a backlog with at least two todo items
2. Run `assist next` — it picks an item and sets it to in-progress
3. In another terminal, run `assist next` again

**Expected:** The second agent skips the item already being worked on and picks the next todo item.

**Actual:** The second agent sees the in-progress item and tries to resume it, competing with the first agent.

**Edge case:** If both agents are terminated without completing, a fresh `assist next` should still pick up one of the abandoned in-progress items.

comments.text: Added PID-based lock files (.assist-lock-{id}.json) so concurrent assist next agents skip items actively worked on by another process. Stale locks (dead PID) are ignored, keeping abandoned items resumable.
PID-based lock files (.assist-lock-{id}.json) prevent concurrent assist next agents from competing for the same item. Stale locks (dead PID) are ignored so abandoned items remain resumable.

name: assist next crashes on Ctrl-C during story prompt

**Expected:** CLI exits gracefully with no output (exit code 0 or 130).

**Actual:** Unhandled promise rejection error is printed to stderr and process exits with a non-zero code.

comments.text: Added exitOnCancel wrapper that catches enquirer Ctrl-C rejections and calls process.exit(0). Applied to all prompt call sites: next.ts select prompt, handleIncompletePhase.ts, promptConfirm, promptInput, promptPassword, and promptMultiselect. Extracted selectItem helper from next.ts to satisfy maintainability threshold.
Added exitOnCancel wrapper for enquirer prompts so Ctrl-C exits cleanly (exit 0) instead of printing an unhandled rejection.

name: Use /api/data endpoint for historical seq queries

The filter expression is translated to SQL WHERE clause (single-quoted strings, Seq SQL syntax)
Results are formatted the same as /api/events output

description:
The Seq /api/events endpoint scans backwards from "now" with a limited scan depth. Even with fromDateUtc, it fails to reach events more than a few hours old. This is a known Seq limitation (see https://github.com/datalust/seq-tickets/discussions/2025).

The /api/data endpoint (SQL query engine) performs a proper indexed search and reliably returns historical events. When --from is specified, seq query should use /api/data instead of /api/events.

Example: `GET /api/data?q=select * from stream where JobId = 'xxx' limit 50&fromDateUtc=2026-03-30T00:00:00.000Z`

description:

description:
The backtick check in splitCompound (`/`.+`/.test(t)`) rejects any token containing backticks, even when they are literal characters inside quoted strings (e.g. markdown formatting in --task values), not shell command substitution.

**Repro:**
1. Run a command with backticks inside quoted arguments, e.g.:

2. The cli-hook check rejects it with "not approved (unable to parse)"

**Expected:** Literal backticks inside quoted arguments should be treated as safe — they are not command substitution.

**Actual:** `splitCompound` returns `undefined`, causing the command to be rejected as unparseable.

name: Phase CLI commands accept 0-based index but display is 1-based
acceptanceCriteria: Web UI PlanSection uses 1-based currentPhase without conversion
description:
All phase display output uses 1-based numbering (e.g. "Phase 1", "Phase 2") but CLI commands like `phase-done`, `rewind`, and `findPhase` accept 0-based indices. This means users and agents see "Phase 2" but must pass `1` to refer to it.

**Repro:**
1. Create a backlog item with a multi-phase plan
2. Run `assist backlog plan <id>` — phases display as Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3
3. Run `assist backlog rewind <id> 2 --reason "test"` to rewind to Phase 2
4. Command fails or targets the wrong phase because it expects 0-based input

**Expected:** Passing `2` targets Phase 2 (the phase displayed as "Phase 2")

**Actual:** Passing `2` targets Phase 3 (the third phase, index 2), or fails validation

comments.text: Phase 1 complete: CLI commands (phase-done, rewind, findPhase) now accept 1-based input. Prompt builders pass 1-based phaseNumber instead of 0-based index. Error messages and comment text use 1-based numbers. Internal storage unchanged.

Review found all 5 AC passing for CLI-level changes, but discovered a critical gap: JSONL export still uses 0-based currentPhase and comment.phase. Agents read the JSONL AND use the CLI, so the mismatch persists at a different boundary.

Decision: make everything 1-based end-to-end — DB storage, BacklogItem type, JSONL, CLI, display. No conversion boundaries. Array accesses get mechanical -1. Requires: DB migration (increment current_phase and comments.phase by 1), update ~15 internal call sites, update tests, update web UI PlanSection phaseStatus comparisons.

Phase 2 review: Implemented full 1-based end-to-end migration. DB migration increments current_phase and comment phase by 1. Removed all +1 display conversions. PlanSection compares index+1 with 1-based currentPhase. CLI commands store 1-based directly. Extracted DB migrations to migrateCommentsAddId.ts to keep openDb.ts maintainability above threshold. All 11 verify commands pass.

Made phase numbering 1-based end-to-end: DB migration increments stored values, removed all +1 display conversions, CLI commands store 1-based directly, PlanSection compares with 1-based currentPhase, run loop converts at boundary.

name: Heredoc syntax bypasses deny rules in CLI hook
acceptanceCriteria: Commands that cannot be decomposed by splitCompound are still checked against deny patterns
python3 << 'EOF' ... EOF is denied when python3 is in the deny list
Existing tests for splitCompound and matchesConfigDeny continue to pass
description:
**Repro:**
1. Configure `assist.yml` with a deny rule for `python3`
2. Have an agent produce a command using heredoc syntax: `python3 << 'EOF' ... EOF`
3. The deny rule is not enforced — the command proceeds to the approval prompt

**Expected:** The command is denied with the message "Do not use python3. Use PowerShell instead."

**Actual:** `splitCompound` returns `undefined` because `<` is in `UNSAFE_OPS`, causing `extractCommand` to return `undefined`, which makes `cliHook` exit without checking deny rules. The command bypasses the deny check entirely.

comments.text: Root cause: spawnRunCommand built a shell string via shellQuote and ran it with spawn(cmd, [], {shell:true}). Fix: pass command and args array directly to spawn() without shell:true, bypassing shell interpretation entirely.

Review: both ACs pass. Fix switches spawn from shell-string mode (shell:true via cmd.exe) to direct argv mode (command+args array), eliminating Windows shell re-interpretation that caused the hang. All run commands (local and linked) share the same code path.

Root cause: two issues. (1) shell:true passes command through cmd.exe which misparses && in bash -c args. Fixed by passing command+args directly to spawn without shell. (2) On Windows from PowerShell, bash resolves to WSL shim (system32\bash.exe) not Git Bash. Fixed by resolving bash to Git's bin\bash.exe via where git. Verified working from both PowerShell and bash.

name: assist run should support suffix matching and show relevant matches on ambiguity
acceptanceCriteria: When a name has no exact match but uniquely matches one config by suffix, that config is executed
When a name matches multiple configs by suffix, only the matching configs are listed
When a name matches nothing (exact or suffix), the full list is shown as before
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have run configurations with prefixed names (e.g. `nextgen:start-proxy`, `poc:start-proxy`)
2. Run `assist run start-proxy`

**Expected:** If the suffix uniquely matches one configuration, run it. If it matches multiple, list only the ambiguous matches (e.g. "Did you mean: `nextgen:start-proxy`, `poc:start-proxy`?").

**Actual:** Prints "No run configuration found" and dumps the entire list of available configurations.

comments.text: Extracted findRunConfig to its own file with suffix matching: unique suffix match runs the config, ambiguous suffix matches list only the matching configs, and no match shows the full list as before.
Suffix matching for assist run: unique suffix match executes config, ambiguous matches list only relevant configs, no match shows full list

name: Agent uses rewind instead of add-phase when asked to add a new phase
acceptanceCriteria: When asked to add a new phase, the agent uses add-phase not rewind
In-flight changes are committed before session ends
description:
**Repro:**
1. Run a backlog item with multiple phases via `assist backlog run <id>`
2. During execution, ask the agent to commit current work and add a new phase before closing out the current one
3. Observe the agent's chosen commands

**Expected:** Agent commits the in-flight changes, then runs `assist backlog add-phase <id> "<phase name>"` to insert the new phase.

**Actual:** Agent skips the commit, runs `assist backlog rewind` to a non-existent phase (gets an error), then rewinds to the current phase and terminates the session. No commit is made, no new phase is added, and session context is lost.

When a repo is selected, discover available run configs and present them in the command picker. Selecting one spawns via the same PTY/WebSocket/session lifecycle. Extracted spawnPty.ts to share PTY spawn logic between spawnClaude and spawnRun. Refactored handleSocket dispatch into handler-map pattern to stay within maintainability threshold.

Added run config discovery via WebSocket (run-configs message returns name+params for a given cwd), generalised PTY spawn into spawnPty.ts shared by spawnClaude/spawnRun, extended session creation with commandType and create-run message, and wired frontend state/actions (runConfigs, createRunSession, requestRunConfigs). History exclusion is inherently handled — run sessions produce no Claude JSONL files.

Phase 2: Frontend integration complete. Run configs are fetched on repo selection change, displayed as additional buttons in the command picker, params render as labeled inputs, and form submission dispatches to createRunSession. Extracted ActiveTab, useNewSessionForm, RunParamInputs, and buildRunArgs for maintainability.

Frontend integration: wired run config fetching on repo change, added run commands to mode selector, rendered param inputs for run commands, and dispatched form submission to createRunSession. Extracted ActiveTab, useNewSessionForm, RunParamInputs components and buildRunArgs helper.

Review: added run filter input, 10-item cap with overflow count, play-symbol + immediate execute for no-param runs, repo prefix on session card names, increased button font size. Extracted multiple small files to satisfy maintainability checks.

Web UI run command execution: discover run configs per repo, show in command picker with wildcard filter and 10-item cap, param inputs for configs with params, immediate execute for no-param runs, PTY spawn via assist run, repo-prefixed session cards, dismiss on exit.
